During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[Atsuta Shrine]]'s ''[[Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]] }} [[sn:Minashi Shrine]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[ja:Minashi Shrine]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine
水無神社 | image = Minashi-jinja.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36|05|7.10|N|137|15|6.7|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Toshigami]] | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[Atsuta Shrine]]'s ''[[Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]] }} [[sn:Minashi Shrine]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[ja:Minashi Shrine]]

From 1874 to 1877, [[Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[Atsuta Shrine]]'s ''[[Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]] [[sn:Minashi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Minashi Shrine]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[ja:Minashi Shrine]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine
水無神社 | image = Minashi-jinja.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36|05|7.10|N|137|15|6.7|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Toshigami]] | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[Atsuta Shrine]]'s ''[[Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minashi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]] [[sn:Minashi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Minashi Shrine]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[ja:Minashi Shrine]]

From 1874 to 1877, [[Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[Atsuta Shrine]]'s ''[[Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine
水無神社 | image = Minashi-jinja.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36|05|7.10|N|137|15|6.7|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Toshigami]] | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[Atsuta Shrine]]'s ''[[Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]] }} [[sn:Minashi Shrine]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[sn:inashi Shrine]] [[sn: failed]]

The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[Atsuta Shrine]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]] }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[sn:Minashi Shrine]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[sn:inashi Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

From 1874 to 1877, [[Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[Atsuta Shrine]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[Atsuta Shrine]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[sn:Minashi Shrine]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[sn:inashi Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine
水無神社 | image = Minashi-jinja.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36|05|7.10|N|137|15|6.7|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Toshigami]] | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = }} '']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[sn:Minashi Shrine]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[sn:inashi Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

From 1874 to 1877, [[Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine
水無神社 | image = Minashi-jinja.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36|05|7.10|N|137|15|6.7|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Toshigami]] | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = }} '']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[sn:Minashi Shrine]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[sn:inashi Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [[sn:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q704865]] Wikidata name: Minashi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine
水無神社 | image = File:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.08530556|N|137.25186111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Mount Kurai|WD=Q6921728}}; {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }} '']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}}

[https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q704865]] Wikidata name: Minashi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine
水無神社 | image = File:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.08530556|N|137.25186111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Mount Kurai|WD=Q6921728}}; {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }} '']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[Atsuta Shrine]]'s ''[[Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[JR Central]] [[Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]] }} [[sn:Minashi Shrine]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[ja:Minashi Shrine]]

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q704865]] Wikidata name: Minashi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine
水無神社 | image = File:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.08530556|N|137.25186111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Mount Kurai|WD=Q6921728}}; {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }} '']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. 水無神社 | image = File:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.08530556|N|137.25186111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Mount Kurai|WD=Q6921728}}; {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }} '']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
