revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q704865]] Wikidata name: Minashi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine
水無神社 | image = File:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.08530556|N|137.25186111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Mount Kurai|WD=Q6921728}}; {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }} '']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank 2]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. [[sn:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q704865]] Wikidata name: Minashi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine
水無神社 | image = File:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.08530556|N|137.25186111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Mount Kurai|WD=Q6921728}}; {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }} '']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}}

The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[sn:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q704865]] Wikidata name: Minashi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine
水無神社 | image = File:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.08530556|N|137.25186111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Mount Kurai|WD=Q6921728}}; {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }} '']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] [[commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q704865]] Wikidata name: Minashi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine
水無神社 | image = File:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.08530556|N|137.25186111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Mount Kurai|WD=Q6921728}}; {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }} '']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[sn:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q704865]] Wikidata name: Minashi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine

It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[sn:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q704865]] Wikidata name: Minashi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine

It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[sn:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q704865]] Wikidata name: Minashi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine
水無神社 | image = File:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.08530556|N|137.25186111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Mount Kurai|WD=Q6921728}}; {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }} '']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seiwa|Emperor Seiwa]] (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era of [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]], the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, [[:en:Shimazaki Masaki|Shimazaki Masaki]], the father of author [[:en:Tōson Shimazaki|Shimazaki Tōson]] and model for the main character of his seminal work [[:en:Before the Dawn (novel)|''Before the Dawn'']] was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from [[:en:Hida-Ichinomiya Station|Hida-Ichinomiya Station]] on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane}}, Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Hida Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q704865]] Wikidata name: Minashi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine
水無神社 | image = File:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.08530556|N|137.25186111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Mount Kurai|WD=Q6921728}}; {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }} '']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|WD=Q622594|lt=Takayama}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|en|Emperor Seiwa|WD=Q349243|lt=Emperor Seiwa}} (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} era of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, {{ill|Shimazaki Masaki|en|Shimazaki Masaki|WD=Q105711327|lt=Shimazaki Masaki}}, the father of author {{ill|Tōson Shimazaki|en|Tōson Shimazaki|WD=Q1093783|lt=Shimazaki Tōson}} and model for the main character of his seminal work {{ill|Before the Dawn (novel)|en|Before the Dawn (novel)|WD=Q4880345|lt=''Before the Dawn''}} was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from {{ill|Hida-Ichinomiya Station|en|Hida-Ichinomiya Station|WD=Q5751478|lt=Hida-Ichinomiya Station}} on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane}}, Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[sn:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q704865]] Wikidata name: Minashi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine

[[co:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q704865]] Wikidata name: Minashi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine
水無神社 | image = File:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.08530556|N|137.25186111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Mount Kurai|WD=Q6921728}}; {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }} '']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|WD=Q622594|lt=Takayama}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|en|Emperor Seiwa|WD=Q349243|lt=Emperor Seiwa}} (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} era of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, {{ill|Shimazaki Masaki|en|Shimazaki Masaki|WD=Q105711327|lt=Shimazaki Masaki}}, the father of author {{ill|Tōson Shimazaki|en|Tōson Shimazaki|WD=Q1093783|lt=Shimazaki Tōson}} and model for the main character of his seminal work {{ill|Before the Dawn (novel)|en|Before the Dawn (novel)|WD=Q4880345|lt=''Before the Dawn''}} was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from {{ill|Hida-Ichinomiya Station|en|Hida-Ichinomiya Station|WD=Q5751478|lt=Hida-Ichinomiya Station}} on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane}}, Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja|Minashi Shrine}} *{{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[file:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg|thumb|right|Minashi Shrine ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] [[en:Minashi Shrine]] {{Commons category|Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja}} [[fr:Minashi-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]] [[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{wikidata link|Q704865}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[sn:commons:Category:Hida-ichinomiya Minashi-jinja]][[en:Minashi Shrine]][[fr:Minashi-jinja]][[ja:飛騨一宮水無神社]][[zh:飛驒一宮水無神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q704865]] Wikidata name: Minashi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Gifu Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Minashi Shrine

It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|en|Emperor Seiwa|WD=Q349243|lt=Emperor Seiwa}} (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} era of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, {{ill|Shimazaki Masaki|en|Shimazaki Masaki|WD=Q105711327|lt=Shimazaki Masaki}}, the father of author {{ill|Tōson Shimazaki|en|Tōson Shimazaki|WD=Q1093783|lt=Shimazaki Tōson}} and model for the main character of his seminal work {{ill|Before the Dawn (novel)|en|Before the Dawn (novel)|WD=Q4880345|lt=''Before the Dawn''}} was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from {{ill|Hida-Ichinomiya Station|en|Hida-Ichinomiya Station|WD=Q5751478|lt=Hida-Ichinomiya Station}} on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane}}, Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. 水無神社 | image = File:Minashi-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Minashi Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.08530556|N|137.25186111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Mount Kurai|WD=Q6921728}}; {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} | founded_by = | established = 9th century | date_destroyed = | location = Takayama-shi, Gifu-ken 502-0071 Japan | website = {{Official|1=http://minashijinjya.or.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = May 2 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }} '']] {{nihongo|'''Hida-Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine''' |飛騨一宮水無神社|Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Jinja}}, commonly: {{nihongo|'''Minashi Shrine'''|水無神社|Minashi Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the Ichinomiya neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|WD=Q622594|lt=Takayama}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the former {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on May 2.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Minashi Jinja is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Toshigami]]'''| 御歳大神 |}}, the god of the year. ==History== There are no official records marking the construction of the shrine; however, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|en|Emperor Seiwa|WD=Q349243|lt=Emperor Seiwa}} (858–878), the shrine appears in official records and was given Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]]. The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, it was referred toes the "Minashi Dai-gongen" and in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} as the "Minashi Myōjin" or the "Minashi Hachiman". During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} era of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}}, the shrine was designated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 3rd Rank |国幣小社|Kokuhei Shosha}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] from 1871. From 1874 to 1877, {{ill|Shimazaki Masaki|en|Shimazaki Masaki|WD=Q105711327|lt=Shimazaki Masaki}}, the father of author {{ill|Tōson Shimazaki|en|Tōson Shimazaki|WD=Q1093783|lt=Shimazaki Tōson}} and model for the main character of his seminal work {{ill|Before the Dawn (novel)|en|Before the Dawn (novel)|WD=Q4880345|lt=''Before the Dawn''}} was the ''[[kannushi]]'' of this shrine.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} Because of the [[:en:Bombing of Nagoya in World War II|Bombing of Nagoya in World War II]], Minashi Shrine served as a refuge for [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]]'s ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi]]'' from August 21 to September 19, 1945. The shrine is located an eight-minute walk from {{ill|Hida-Ichinomiya Station|en|Hida-Ichinomiya Station|WD=Q5751478|lt=Hida-Ichinomiya Station}} on the [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]] {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Ichinomiyamachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 509-3505, Japan - panoramio (2).jpg|Approach Minashi-jinja.jpeg|Precincts Minashi jinja kuhi.JPG|Memorial to Shimazaki Masaki ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe.
